avoid: Do not write a custom full GEMM for W @ X.
Do not prioritize block-size or float4 micro-tuning of the scalar rank-16 kernel before validating the pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM baseline.
Do not add TORCH_CHECK validation, contiguity copies, dtype checks, or explicit d-range checks to the hot path under trusted-input benchmark assumptions.
Do not materialize B.T as a separate tensor.
Do not use ATen matmul as a fallback unless correctness fails and no cuBLAS repair is available.
Do not promote based on aggregate speedup when one supported shape regresses substantially.
evidence: Correctness passes for all tested d values with max_abs_err 0.00146484375 and rel_l2_err 7.9e-7, so there is no immediate correctness blocker.
Aggregate speedup is only 1.002x, effectively neutral versus torch baseline.
Performance is inconsistent by shape: d=3584 improves 1.044x and d=4608 improves 1.044x, but d=4096 regresses to 0.940x.

The candidate uses a custom deterministic rank-16 update; the profile labels the bottleneck as rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth, suggesting the handwritten low-rank path is not reliably beating library SGEMM.
W @ X is the dominant d x d GEMM term, so the main multiplication should remain direct cuBLAS SGEMM rather than a custom full GEMM.
Static warnings about no dtype/contiguity/TORCH_CHECK validation are acceptable for the desired trusted-input fast path and are not the cause of the observed runtime regression.

recommended_actions: try float4 stores when d is divisible by 4
fall back to ATen if numerical error appears
Do not promote this version as-is because the d=4096 regression is large enough to erase aggregate benefit.
Replace the handwritten rank-16 update with the minimal pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation based on /workspace/lora/3_sgemm.cu.
Use direct cuBLAS SGEMM for the main term W @ X.

Use the exact low-rank cuBLAS sequence: SGEMM1 opA=N/opB=N with A=X and B=W to produce Y; SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T with A=X and B=B, m=d, n=16, k=d, output temporary U shape {d,16}, ldc=d; SGEMM3 opA=N/opB=N with A=U and B=A, m=d, n=d, k=16, beta=1 to accumulate into Y.
Avoid explicit B.T materialization; use cuBLAS transpose flag for B in the second SGEMM.

Keep the trusted-input fast path minimal: CUDAGuard, getCurrentCUDABlasHandle, direct tensor.data_ptr<float>() inside cublasSgemm, no validation checks, no input copies, no explicit d-range checks, and no ATen matmul.

If this custom-rank16 approach is retained for exploration, use shape-aware dispatch only after separately validating that it improves specific d values without regressing others; otherwise fall back to the pure cuBLAS path for all shapes.
